subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF Art IV 2025, World Bank, Freedom House, RSF, HRW, Amnesty, CPJ, non-Saudi press); Saudi state/state-aligned media (SPA, Al Arabiya, Arab News) EXCLUDED there, with economic figures admitted only via IMF validation.
Governance: an absolute monarchy; scores reflect observable actions (record executions, the NEOM scaleback, PIF spending cuts, peg mechanics), not Vision-2030 messaging.
Standout drags: no political rights and severe repression, no independent media, a hard oil-transition and water/heat tail; offsets are sound banks, very low crime, and a young population.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), a fragile/violated 8 Apr ceasefire, and the global energy shock. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: Sound banks, low inflation, and a large sovereign-fund buffer, against oil dependence and a peg that surrenders monetary autonomy.

fiscal_state.notes: War spending plus the Hormuz-driven export/revenue disruption widen the deficit despite high prices. (IMF Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: A hard USD peg means no autonomous policy and imports the US rate cycle - a stability-for-autonomy trade-off. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The peg delivers FX stability for holders, backed by ample reserves. (IMF)

inflation.notes: Low (~2.2%), with rent inflation decelerating. (IMF Art IV)

banking_stability.notes: Well-capitalised (a capital ratio ~19.6%) with low NPLs. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Most crude exports transit the blockaded Strait of Hormuz; the ~5 mbd East-West (Petroline) pipeline to Yanbu gives a partial Red Sea bypass that cushions but cannot offset the shock. Recovers if the strait reopens. (Reuters; EIA)

institutional.notes: High state capacity and megaproject execution, against no judicial independence and the absence of independent media.

rule_of_law.notes: No judicial independence in an absolute monarchy with Sharia courts; functional commercial-law reforms offset weak rights protection - net negative. (Freedom House; WJP)

statistical_integrity.notes: Economic statistics are IMF-validated; political and social data are opaque. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: High state capacity and megaproject execution despite scalebacks. (IMF; World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: No independent media, a very low RSF standing, and the Khashoggi legacy; a journalist was executed in 2025. (RSF; CPJ)

political_social.notes: A young population, against the absence of political rights, severe repression, and a rights-deprived migrant workforce.

political_stability.notes: High managed/suppressed stability under the crown prince near-term, against long-term succession and oil-transition risk. (Freedom House)

civil_liberties.notes: No political rights, with guardianship reforms outweighed by ongoing repression and among the world's highest execution counts. (HRW; Amnesty; Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Easy iqama/premium-residency entry and no income tax, but no path to citizenship, kafala-legacy controls, and a large rights-deprived expatriate workforce. (HRW; Amnesty)

social_cohesion.notes: Orderly with low crime, but built on a structurally rights-deprived migrant-majority workforce. (HRW)

demographics.notes: A young Saudi population (a dividend), tempered by labour-nationalisation friction. (World Bank; IMF)

geopolitical.notes: A transactional US partner with OPEC+ leverage, an open capital account, and a holding Iran detente.

alliance_reliability.notes: A US security partner hedging China and Russia, with OPEC+ leverage - transactional but stable. (Soufan Center)

conflict_involvement.notes: Struck by Iranian missile/drone waves; opened King Fahd Air Base to US forces and edged toward co-belligerency in the 2026 Iran war. (WSJ; Al Jazeera)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open capital account with free profit and capital movement. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Modern healthcare and infrastructure and very low crime, against extreme heat and water scarcity long-term.

climate.notes: Extreme heat (Hajj wet-bulb risk), desert water scarcity, desal dependence, with a wealth/readiness partial offset. (IPCC AR6; WB CCDR)

healthcare.notes: Good modern urban care and improving. (WHO; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: World-class build-out, but desalination supplies most drinking water and Iran has both threatened Gulf water plants and hit them before (Houthis, 2022) — an acute near-term water-security vulnerability. (CSIS; Atlantic Council)

crime_safety.notes: Very low violent crime - a clear strength. (international advisories)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low natural-catastrophe exposure beyond heat and flash floods. (reinsurer risk data)

summary: An oil-rich economy with sound, well-capitalised banks, low inflation, very low crime, modern urban infrastructure and healthcare, a large sovereign-fund buffer, and a young population. But it is an absolute monarchy: no political rights, severe repression and among the world's highest execution counts, no independent media, and a workforce resting on a structurally rights-deprived expatriate majority. A USD peg surrenders monetary autonomy for stability, and the long-term tail is a hard oil transition atop extreme heat and water scarcity. Easy residency and no income tax come with no path to belonging.
